title: Sequence

publication.changeSummary: Topic-specific draft for Sequence; requires human source, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Software Development Fundamentals; August 2023; section applicable to Sequence
sources.claim: Sequential statements complete in a defined order, and each statement reads the state produced by earlier statements before creating the next state.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Sequential statements complete in a defined order, and each statement reads the state produced by earlier statements before creating the next state.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A receipt routine reads price, computes tax from price, then computes total from both values; moving total before tax breaks its dependency. Your goal is to produce a correct state table for sequential pseudocode. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Expressions and evaluation. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Operational model
sections.body: Sequential statements complete in a defined order, and each statement reads the state produced by earlier statements before creating the next state. The learner should use this model to predict an observable result rather than merely repeat terminology.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure point
sections.body: Function calls, exceptions, asynchronous work, and concurrency can interrupt or defer apparent textual order, so this model applies to the declared synchronous path. A correct explanation therefore includes the applicable assumptions and avoids this common mistake: Independent-looking lines may be freely reordered because a program eventually executes all of them anyway.

includes: Trace ordered statements and identify dependencies between them.
Produce a correct state table for sequential pseudocode.
excludes: Function calls, exceptions, asynchronous work, and concurrency can interrupt or defer apparent textual order, so this model applies to the declared synchronous path.

outcomes: Produce a correct state table for sequential pseudocode.

examples.title: Sequence: direct case
examples.situation: A receipt routine reads price, computes tax from price, then computes total from both values; moving total before tax breaks its dependency.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the starting evidence: A receipt routine reads price, computes tax from price, then computes total from both values; moving total before tax breaks its dependency.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Check the result against this correction: Preserve data and effect dependencies by tracing the state after each statement and moving a statement only when its reads and writes remain valid.
examples.result: The case supports the declared outcome because Preserve data and effect dependencies by tracing the state after each statement and moving a statement only when its reads and writes remain valid.

examples.title: Sequence: boundary case
examples.situation: A conversion routine parses text before multiplying the number and formats output only after the calculation succeeds.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed context: A conversion routine parses text before multiplying the number and formats output only after the calculation succeeds.

Step 2 — Keep this boundary explicit: Function calls, exceptions, asynchronous work, and concurrency can interrupt or defer apparent textual order, so this model applies to the declared synchronous path.
Step 3 — Reject the documented misconception: Independent-looking lines may be freely reordered because a program eventually executes all of them anyway.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Produce a correct state table for sequential pseudocode.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Independent-looking lines may be freely reordered because a program eventually executes all of them anyway.
misconceptions.correction: Preserve data and effect dependencies by tracing the state after each statement and moving a statement only when its reads and writes remain valid.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using the direct and boundary scenarios, explain why this belief fails: Independent-looking lines may be freely reordered because a program eventually executes all of them anyway.

analogy.example: A route with ordered checkpoints, forks, and repeated laps can model Sequence while each decision uses the current conditions.
usefulFor: Visualizing order, branching, repetition, progress, and nested routes
limitations: Real control flow can include calls, exceptions, concurrency, and evaluation rules absent from a physical route.
Function calls, exceptions, asynchronous work, and concurrency can interrupt or defer apparent textual order, so this model applies to the declared synchronous path.

recap.summary: You should now be able to produce a correct state table for sequential pseudocode. Keep this boundary in view: Function calls, exceptions, asynchronous work, and concurrency can interrupt or defer apparent textual order, so this model applies to the declared synchronous path.
retrievalPrompts: Explain Sequence without using the optional analogy and include one observable consequence.
Why is this statement incorrect: Independent-looking lines may be freely reordered because a program eventually executes all of them anyway.
Apply the model to this case from memory: A conversion routine parses text before multiplying the number and formats output only after the calculation succeeds.
recap.nextReviewPrompt: Next: continue to “Selection”. Before moving on, answer one retrieval prompt without reopening the explanation.
